steps.title: Calculate the slope from two points
steps.content:
With points $(-2, 3)$ and $(4, -5)$, substitute into the slope formula:
$$m = \frac{-5 - 3}{4 - (-2)} = \frac{-8}{6} = -\frac{4}{3}$$

steps.title: Find the y-intercept and write the equation
steps.content:
Substitute the slope $-\frac{4}{3}$ and point $(4, -5)$ into $y = mx + b$:
$$-5 = -\frac{4}{3}(4) + b$$
$$-5 = -\frac{16}{3} + b$$
$$b = -5 + \frac{16}{3} = \frac{1}{3}$$
The equation is $f(x) = -\frac{4}{3}x + \frac{1}{3}$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
